findings.source_file: Узел Питания и управления_Узел Питания и управления.kicad_sch.json
findings.summary: Industrial food processing machine power/control unit (363 components, 25 sub-sheets, 12 IRF4905 MOSFET circuits). Major classification issue: lib_id ignored in favor of reference prefix, causing 29 LED-strip connectors as LEDs, 6 circuit breakers as capacitors, 2 DS18B20 sensors as transformers, 1 AC motor as capacitor.

correct.description: 12 IRF4905 P-channel MOSFETs correctly identified with is_pchannel=true
24 TVS diodes and 25 fuses correctly identified as protection_devices
363 components and 669 nets correctly extracted across 25+ sheets

incorrect.description: T1/T2 (DS18B20, lib_id Sensor_Temperature:DS18B20) misclassified as transformer due to T prefix
29 LED-strip connectors (lib_id Connector:Conn_01x01_Pin) misclassified as LED due to LED prefix in reference
6 circuit breakers CB1-CB6 (lib_id Device:CircuitBreaker) misclassified as capacitor due to CB prefix
COMPR (lib_id Motor:Motor_AC, compressor) misclassified as capacitor due to C prefix
QF1 (lib_id Device:Residual_current_device, RCD/GFCI) misclassified as transistor due to Q prefix
V_SATA_SSD (lib_id Connector:Conn_01x01_Pin) misclassified as varistor due to V prefix

missed.description: 12 SB560 flyback diodes across lock solenoids not detected (has_flyback_diode=false on all MOSFETs)
R1-R12 gate resistors not detected for MOSFET circuits due to hierarchical net tracing gaps
RS-485 bus protocol not detected despite RS485 nets and dedicated sub-sheet

suggestions: Prioritize lib_id over reference prefix for type classification — Connector:* should always be connector, Sensor_Temperature:* not transformer, Device:CircuitBreaker not capacitor, Motor:* not capacitor
Fix net tracing in hierarchical sheets to detect flyback diodes and gate resistors for MOSFET switches
findings.project: Узел Питания и управления_Узел Питания и управления
